meta_title: For Founders: deployment workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Deployment Workflow to Solve Project Inertia
content.seo_description: Stop starting and start shipping. Use this expert deployment workflow with Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your SaaS MVP and hit revenue faster.
content.answer_block: Inertia kills SaaS products. You finish projects by automating the feedback loop, not just the code. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative deployment, you collapse the distance between local development and live revenue, ensuring your MVP reaches users before your motivation wanes.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-finished' projects. You start with a burst of dopamine-fueled energy, but as the complexity of deployment settles in, the momentum dies. You aren't failing because you lack ideas; you are failing because your infrastructure is fighting your velocity.

Real founders don't obsess over perfection; they obsess over shipping. By adopting a hardened deployment workflow, you stop treating deployment as a final hurdle and start treating it as a continuous heartbeat. This checklist is your blueprint to turn your local prototype into a live, scalable product that generates revenue.

steps.title: Define the Lean Infrastructure
steps.description: Establish a minimal viable environment that prioritizes speed-to-market over premature scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lean SaaS boilerplate using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe. Focus on a modular architecture that allows for rapid feature swapping.

steps.title: Automate the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Configure your environment so that every commit triggers a deploy, allowing you to see changes in production instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automates deployment to Vercel upon every push to the main branch.

steps.title: Implement Feature Flagging
steps.description: Decouple deployment from release to ship code safely even when features are incomplete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a simple feature flag service to toggle experimental features without rolling back the entire build.

steps.title: Database Schema Synchronization
steps.description: Avoid deployment friction by automating schema migrations during the build process.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a migration script for Supabase that ensures local schema changes are pushed to production seamlessly.

steps.title: Continuous Monitoring Integration
steps.description: Don't fly blind. Integrate error tracking so you know exactly when a user hits a bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add Sentry integration to the error boundary of the application to track production runtime errors.

pro_tips: Never deploy on a Friday; use feature flags to push code that stays hidden until you are ready to reveal.
Treat your database as the source of truth, not your UI state; build migrations into your CI pipeline early.
If it takes more than 3 minutes to deploy, you have over-engineered your build process; simplify or move to serverless.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it, ensuring your future self doesn't have to decipher your logic.
Prioritize shipping a 'broken' feature that provides value over a 'perfect' feature that nobody can access yet.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, immediately set up a feedback channel (Discord or Typeform) on your landing page. Do not write another line of code until you have validated your MVP with at least three paying users.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the strategy. The only thing missing is your execution. Stop stalling and start shipping your vision to the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
